revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:慈雲寺DVC00131.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625|1=Rinzai school}}|prefecture={{ill|Shimosuwa|qid=Q1204211|1=Shimosuwa}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.08266666666667|N|138.08363888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Yishan Yining|qid=Q2499160|1=Yishan Yining}}}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Jiun-ji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = | location = Higashimachi-naka 606, Shimosuwa, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture, Japan | latitude_deg = 36 | latitude_min = 04 | latitude_sec = 57.6 | longitude_deg = 138 | longitude_min = 05 | longitude_sec = 01.1 | sect = {{ill|Myoshin-ji School of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshin-ji School of Rinzai Zen|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108821667}} | deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|1=Ekādaśamukha}} Bodhisattva | founded = {{ill|1300|en|1300|qid=Q5609|lt=1300|1=1300}} (Shōan 2) | founder = {{ill|Yishan Yining|en|Yishan Yining|qid=Q2499160|lt=Issan Ichinei|1=Yishan Yining}} (founder), Kinasa Tōtōmi-no-kami Mitsusada (patron) | full_name = Hakugazan Jiun-ji | designation = Nagano Prefectural Treasure (Temple Bell) | website = | map = Nagano Prefecture | map_relief = yes | map_width = 256 }} '''Jiun-ji''' (慈雲寺) is a {{ill|Myoshin-ji School of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗妙心寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoshin-ji School of Rinzai Zen|1=Myōshin-ji sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108821667}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|qid=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is '''Hakugazan''' (白華山), and its principal object of worship is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Avalokitesvara|1=Ekādaśamukha}}.
== History == Jiun-ji was established in {{ill|1300|en|1300|qid=Q5609|lt=1300|1=1300}} (Shōan 2) as part of the {{ill|Kencho-ji School of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji School of Rinzai Zen|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110991919}} lineage. It was founded by {{ill|Yishan Yining|en|Yishan Yining|qid=Q2499160|lt=Issan Ichinei|1=Yishan Yining}}, with {{ill|Kinasa Mitsusada|ja|金刺満貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinasa Mitsusada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the head priest of {{ill|Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as its patron. Issan was succeeded by Setsusun Yūbai, and the temple became a prominent Zen center in the region.
Jiun-ji played a significant role in the region's religious and cultural history, serving as the ''shuchū'' (headquarters) for local Zen temples. It was favored by [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who admired the temple's seventh abbot, Tenkai Genchō, and adopted the temple's crest, the Takeda clan's diamond-shaped emblem.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Nagano Prefectural Treasure''' ** '''Temple Bell''' - Cast in 1368 (Ōan 1). The bell measures 114 cm in height and 66.4 cm in diameter. * '''Town-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** '''Main Hall''' - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5) by master carpenter Ichizō of Suwa. Constructed in the {{ill|Tatekawa style|ja|立川流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa style|1=Q11597929|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597929}}. ** '''Sanmon Gate''' - Built in 1779 (An'ei 8) by carpenter Chōzaemon Murata. ** '''Amitabha Buddha Standing Statue''' - Carved by Mokujiki, standing 112 cm tall, dated to 1807 (Bunka 4). ** '''Memorial Pagoda for Hineno Takayoshi''' - A five-ring pagoda commemorating Hineno Takayoshi, the first lord of the Suwa Domain.
== Access == * The temple is approximately a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shimo-Suwa Station|en|Shimo-Suwa Station|qid=Q7496937|lt=Shimosuwa Station|1=Shimo-Suwa Station}} on the JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}.
== Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine Spring Pavilion|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Lower Shrine Spring Pavilion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Manji-no-sekibutsu|ja|万治の石仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manji-no-sekibutsu|1=Manji Stone Buddha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353878}}
== References == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines'' (1981). Shinano Mainichi Shimbun. * ''Exploring Shinshu's Ancient Zen Temples'' (1996). Kyodo Publishing.
== External Links == * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shrine and Buddhism Project] - A project exploring the history of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}, syncretism, and separation of Shinto and Buddhism.
== See Also == * {{ill|Shimosuwa Landmarks|ja|下諏訪町の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Landmarks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}} {{buddhism-stub}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}

[[image:慈雲寺DVC00131.JPG|200px|Jiun-ji]]
[[ja:慈雲寺 (長野県下諏訪町)]]
